comment_text.sn19.1:1.1: In this collection, a monk with psychic power sees beings invisible to the normal eye who are suffering due to their bad deeds in past lives. The Buddha confirms the vision, and explains the reason why they are experiencing that particular form of suffering. In each case the punishment fits the crime, although it is not always obvious how, so I note the connections.
comment_text.sn19.1:1.3: Lakkhaṇa (“Mark”) appears only here and in the corresponding passage at Bu Pj 4:9.1.1. He sees the “marks” by which the results of deeds are manifest, a topic elsewhere treated under the “marks” of a Great Man (DN 30). I translate the name to emphasize its artifice.
comment_text.sn19.1:3.2: The verbs here are uncertain and readings vary considerably. See MN 54:16.1, and compare Rig Veda 6.46.14 where horse-racers compete “like birds over raw flesh”.
comment_text.sn19.1:3.7: Here yakkha is used as a generic term for “spirit”, “entity”.
comment_text.sn19.1:4.7: As a butcher, he is treated like a piece of meat (MN 54:16.1). The same applies to the next three discourses.

root_text.sn19.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn19.1:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā ca lakkhaṇo āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno gijjhakūṭe pabbate viharanti.
root_text.sn19.1:1.4: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yenāyasmā lakkhaṇo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ lakkhaṇaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn19.1:1.6: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā lakkhaṇo āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa paccassosi.
root_text.sn19.1:1.7: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto aññatarasmiṁ padese sitaṁ pātvākāsi.
root_text.sn19.1:1.8: Atha kho āyasmā lakkhaṇo āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn19.1:1.9: “ko nu kho, āvuso moggallāna, hetu ko paccayo sitassa pātukammāyā”ti?
root_text.sn19.1:1.11: Bhagavato maṁ santike etaṁ pañhaṁ pucchā”ti.
root_text.sn19.1:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā ca lakkhaṇo āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno rājagahe piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā lakkhaṇo āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn19.1:2.2: “idhāyasmā mahāmoggallāno gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto aññatarasmiṁ padese sitaṁ pātvākāsi.
root_text.sn19.1:2.3: Ko nu kho, āvuso moggallāna, hetu ko paccayo sitassa pātukammāyā”ti?
root_text.sn19.1:3.1: “Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasaṁ aṭṭhikasaṅkhalikaṁ vehāsaṁ gacchantiṁ.
root_text.sn19.1:3.2: Tamenaṁ gijjhāpi kākāpi kulalāpi anupatitvā anupatitvā phāsuḷantarikāhi vitudenti vitacchenti virājenti.
root_text.sn19.1:4.3: ñāṇabhūtā vata, bhikkhave, sāvakā viharanti, yatra hi nāma sāvako evarūpaṁ ñassati vā dakkhati vā sakkhiṁ vā karissati.
root_text.sn19.1:4.4: Pubbeva me so, bhikkhave, satto diṭṭho ahosi, api cāhaṁ na byākāsiṁ.
root_text.sn19.1:4.5: Ahañcetaṁ byākareyyaṁ, pare ca me na saddaheyyuṁ.
root_text.sn19.1:4.6: Ye me na saddaheyyuṁ, tesaṁ taṁ assa dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
root_text.sn19.1:4.7: Eso, bhikkhave, satto imasmiṁyeva rājagahe goghātako ahosi.
root_text.sn19.1:4.8: So tassa kammassa vipākena bahūni vassāni bahūni vassasatāni bahūni vassasahassāni bahūni vassasatasahassāni niraye paccitvā tasseva kammassa vipākāvasesena evarūpaṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhaṁ paṭisaṁvedayatī”ti.

translation_text.sn19.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn19.1:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Mark and Venerable Mahāmoggallāna were staying on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.sn19.1:1.4: Then Mahāmoggallāna robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to Mark and said to him,
translation_text.sn19.1:1.5: “Come, Reverend Mark, let’s enter Rājagaha for alms.”
translation_text.sn19.1:1.7: As Mahāmoggallāna was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain he smiled at a certain spot.
translation_text.sn19.1:1.9: “What is the cause, Reverend Moggallāna, what is the reason you smiled?”
translation_text.sn19.1:1.10: “Reverend Mark, it’s the wrong time for this question.
translation_text.sn19.1:1.11: Ask me when we’re in the Buddha’s presence.”
translation_text.sn19.1:2.1: Then Mark and Mahāmoggallāna wandered for alms in Rājagaha. After the meal, on their return from almsround, they went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. Mark said to Mahāmoggallāna:
translation_text.sn19.1:2.2: “Just now, as Mahāmoggallāna was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain he smiled at a certain spot.
translation_text.sn19.1:2.3: What is the cause, Reverend Moggallāna, what is the reason you smiled?”
translation_text.sn19.1:3.1: “Just now, reverend, as I was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain I saw a skeleton flying through the air.
translation_text.sn19.1:3.2: Vultures, crows, and hawks kept chasing it, pecking, clawing, and stabbing it in the ribs
translation_text.sn19.1:3.6: That there can be such a sentient being,
translation_text.sn19.1:4.1: Then the Buddha said to the mendicants:
translation_text.sn19.1:4.2: “Mendicants, there are disciples who live full of vision and knowledge,
translation_text.sn19.1:4.3: since a disciple knows, sees, and witnesses such a thing.
translation_text.sn19.1:4.4: Formerly, I too saw that being, but I did not speak of it.
translation_text.sn19.1:4.5: For if I had spoken of it others would not have believed me,
translation_text.sn19.1:4.6: which would be for their lasting harm and suffering.
translation_text.sn19.1:4.7: That being used to be a cattle butcher right here in Rājagaha.
translation_text.sn19.1:4.8: As a result of that deed he burned in hell for many years, many hundreds, many thousands, many hundreds of thousands of years. Now he experiences the residual result of that deed in such an incarnation.”
translation_text.sn19.1:4.9: (Tell all these discourses in full like this.)

variant_text.sn19.1:3.2: virājenti → vitudenti (bj); vitacchenti vibhajenti (pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
variant_text.sn19.1:4.5: Ahañcetaṁ → ahamevetaṁ (bj) | pare ca me → pare me (bj); parepi me (sya-all)
